ground_truth.title: 2025 Best Data Analytics Schools
ground_truth.main_content: College Factual reviewed 34 schools in the United States to determine which ones were the best for degree seekers in the field of data analytics. When you put them all together, these colleges and universities awarded 3,495 degrees in data analytics annually. The data analytics program you select can have a big impact on your future. That's why we developed our collection of Best Schools for Data Analytics rankings. We derive our Best Overall Data Analytics School rankings by rolling up our degree-level rankings after weighting them by the number of degrees awarded at each school. The top-ranked schools for data analytics are: University of Pennsylvania in Philadelphia, PA (ranked #1); Carnegie Mellon University in Pittsburgh, PA (ranked #2); Columbia University in the City of New York in New York, NY (ranked #3); New York University in New York, NY (ranked #4); University of Minnesota - Twin Cities in Minneapolis, MN (ranked #5); University of Michigan - Ann Arbor in Ann Arbor, MI (ranked #6); University of Pittsburgh - Pittsburgh Campus in Pittsburgh, PA (ranked #7); Drexel University in Philadelphia, PA (ranked #8); University of Delaware in Newark, DE (ranked #9); Fordham University in Bronx, NY (ranked #10). The page also lists regional rankings by area including Southeast, New England, Rocky Mountains, Plains States, Middle Atlantic, Far Western US, Southwest, Other U.S. Territories, and Great Lakes.
with: College Factual reviewed 34 schools in the United States to determine which ones were the best for degree seekers in the field of data analytics.
When you put them all together, these colleges and universities awarded 3,495 degrees in data analytics annually.
The data analytics program you select can have a big impact on your future. That's why we developed our collection of Best Schools for Data Analytics rankings.
Located in the large city of Philadelphia, UPenn is a private not-for-profit university with a very large student population.

The Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS) from the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES), a branch of the U.S. Department of Education (DOE) serves as the core of the rest of our data about colleges.
